channel.title: Multi-Blog Framework Demo
channel.description: Recent content on Multi-Blog Framework Demo

item.title: Sunny Morning Pho on Spadina
item.description:
Took my sister there for a quick lunch on a Tuesday. It’s a narrow place, maybe 18 seats, and the broth is the reason to go. They simmer it overnight and you can taste the difference between this and the chain pho places in the same neighborhood. The brisket was clean. The condiment plate was generous.

One thing to know: they only take cash or e-transfer. Walked in expecting tap and had to find an ATM around the corner.

item.title: Hugo's per-section RSS is the multi-blog feature
item.description:
Most static-site generators give you one RSS feed for the whole site. Hugo gives you one per section, and that turns out to be the whole game when you want to run multiple distinct blogs from one repo.

The relevant config:

outputs:
 home: [HTML, RSS]
 section: [HTML, RSS]

With that set, every directory under content/ becomes its own publication with its own feed. A reader who only cares about your tech notes can subscribe to /tech-notes/index.xml and never see your book reviews. The feeds carry independent metadata, so each “blog” can have its own author and description in the RSS envelope without leaking into the others.

item.title: The Dispossessed, Ursula K. Le Guin
item.description:
Read this on a flight back from a client visit and finished it the next morning at the kitchen table. It’s about a physicist defecting from a moon colony to the planet his ancestors fled, and it’s structured as alternating chapters, one timeline going forward, one going backward, meeting in the middle.

The structural trick is the part everyone praises and it works. But the thing that stuck with me was the politics. Le Guin makes both societies look livable and both look corrupting, and she does it without ever telling you which one she prefers. That’s harder than it sounds. Most political novels lose their nerve in the third act and pick a side. She just keeps showing you the trade-offs.
